pageMeta.title: AI Agents for Robotics Integration Support
pageMeta.description: DocsBot helps robotics integration teams answer technical questions, qualify projects, and reduce documentation bottlenecks with grounded AI agents.

hero.headline: DocsBot AI agents for faster robotics integration support
hero.subheadline: Turn robot manuals, PLC notes, commissioning checklists, service SOPs, proposals, and support history into AI agents that help customers, field technicians, sales engineers, and project teams find the right answer before work stalls.

proofBullets.text: Answer customer and technician questions from approved robot manuals, cell documentation, FAQs, safety notes, and internal procedures.
Collect project requirements, qualify automation-fit conversations, and route qualified opportunities to sales or engineering workflows.
Use configured AI Actions to summarize tickets, create handoff notes, look up approved record status, or trigger follow-up workflows.
Surface recurring unanswered questions so integration teams can improve manuals, training content, and customer onboarding resources.

businessClarity.whatDocsBotDoes: DocsBot helps robotics integration companies create AI agents trained on their own technical knowledge. Agents can answer source-grounded questions, support presales and service teams, summarize context, identify documentation gaps, and take bounded actions through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, MCP options, or workflow automations.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Support teams handle recurring questions about robot operation, alarms, preventative maintenance, spare parts, documentation, and escalation paths. DocsBot gives them a first-line AI support agent grounded in approved sources.

bullets.text: Draft and summarize helpdesk responses from manuals, troubleshooting guides, previous ticket exports, and internal SOPs.
Route high-impact, warranty, safety, or site-specific issues to a human with a concise conversation summary and collected details.
Use analytics and conversation review to spot repeated gaps in manuals, commissioning docs, and support articles.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Engineering teams need fast access to robot specs, end-effector notes, PLC conventions, IO maps, risk-assessment references, and integration lessons from earlier projects.

bullets.text: Ask private technical questions across PDFs, Word documents, wikis, Confluence pages, Google Drive folders, and internal notes.
Retrieve consistent answers about procedures, tool choices, internal standards, and project documentation without searching file shares.
Keep safety-critical judgments with engineers while using AI to find approved references, summarize context, and prepare review notes.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Robotics buyers often arrive with incomplete requirements, facility constraints, production targets, and compatibility questions. DocsBot can help presales teams collect the right context before a human designs the solution.

bullets.text: Answer fit, specification, service, pricing-policy, implementation, and timeline questions from approved sales enablement materials.
Collect lead details such as application, payload, cycle-time target, facility constraints, budget range, and preferred follow-up channel.
Forward qualified lead summaries to CRM, email, forms, or team notifications through approved customer-configured workflows.

whoItHelps.roleContext: As more robots are deployed, integrators must keep field teams, new hires, and customers aligned on changing procedures and documentation.

bullets.text: IFR reported 542,000 industrial robot installations worldwide in 2024, more than double the annual count from ten years earlier.
Create always-available onboarding and training support from internal playbooks, commissioning guides, checklists, and customer handover materials.
Review employee and customer questions to identify where training content is unclear, missing, or outdated.

evidenceSources.claim: The International Federation of Robotics reported that global industrial robot installations reached 542,000 units in 2024, more than double the count from ten years earlier.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: World Robotics 2025 report – INDUSTRIAL ROBOTS – released by IFR

evidenceSources.metric: 393,700 industrial robots working in U.S. factories
evidenceSources.claim: IFR reported 393,700 industrial robots working in U.S. factories in 2024, with annual U.S. installations reaching 34,200 units.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: U.S. Lags China in Factory Robot Deployment by 5 to 1 Ratio

evidenceSources.metric: Workers may enter a robot working envelope during operations
evidenceSources.claim: OSHA notes that workers may temporarily be inside a robot working envelope during some operations, where unintended operations can result in injuries.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Robotics - Overview | Occupational Safety and Health Administration

evidenceSources.metric: Integrators and nearby workers need to understand robot hazards and safeguards
evidenceSources.claim: OSHA’s Technical Manual states that integrators, operators, maintenance workers, and others working near robot applications need to understand the nature and severity of hazards and how safeguards address them.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: OSHA Technical Manual, Section IV, Chapter 4: Industrial Robot Systems and Industrial Robot System Safety

evidenceSources.metric: North American robot orders and adoption trends are tracked by A3
evidenceSources.claim: The Association for Advancing Automation tracks North American robot order trends and reported increased adoption by non-automotive companies in its industry statistics coverage.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Robotics Industry Statistics - Association for Advancing Automation

agentUseCases.name: Technical support agent for installed robot cells
agentUseCases.problem: Customers and field technicians ask repetitive but high-detail questions about alarms, maintenance intervals, startup steps, tooling, spare parts, and documentation locations.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers from approved manuals, commissioning packages, service SOPs, knowledge bases, and ticket exports. When the answer needs a person, the agent collects robot model, cell ID, alarm code, operating context, and attempted fixes, then prepares a handoff summary.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With configured Skills or APIs, the agent can look up approved case status, create a support ticket, route a request, notify a team, or attach a conversation summary to a workflow.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What does this alarm code mean on the palletizing cell?
Where is the preventative maintenance checklist for the EOAT vacuum system?
Create a support handoff for this downtime issue with the details I entered.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Reduce repetitive support effort while giving customers and technicians faster access to the documentation your team already trusts.

agentUseCases.name: Presales qualification for automation projects
agentUseCases.problem: Prospects often ask whether a robot can handle a product, payload, takt time, floor-space constraint, vision requirement, or integration budget before a sales engineer can scope the opportunity.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A DocsBot presales agent answers from service pages, case studies, specification sheets, proposal templates, FAQs, and sales playbooks. It can collect structured requirements and apply customer-defined qualification rules for fit, urgency, region, budget, and project readiness.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Approved workflows can forward qualified lead summaries to CRM, email, forms, scheduling tools, or a sales-engineering queue. Humans remain responsible for final pricing, feasibility, and contractual commitments.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Can you automate case packing for mixed SKUs?
What information do you need before quoting a cobot tending project?
Book a discovery call and include my payload, cycle time, and available floor space.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Help sales teams prioritize better-scoped opportunities and reduce back-and-forth before the first engineering conversation.

agentUseCases.name: Internal commissioning and SOP knowledge agent
agentUseCases.problem: Commissioning teams lose time searching through file shares, prior project folders, vendor PDFs, PLC notes, and internal procedures when they need fast answers on-site.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot trains on private internal knowledge sources such as SOPs, PDFs, Word documents, wikis, Google Drive, Notion, Confluence, and text files. Engineers can ask questions in private chat and receive source-grounded answers with links back to approved references.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: If configured, Skills can retrieve approved project metadata, summarize the latest handover notes, create a task, or trigger a review workflow for missing documentation.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What is our standard FAT checklist for an integrated robot cell?
Summarize the handoff notes for this packaging-line project.
Where do we document customer acceptance exceptions?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Help field and engineering teams reuse institutional knowledge without relying on the one person who remembers where a file lives.

agentUseCases.name: Safety documentation and training assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Robotics integration work involves hazard analysis, safeguarding, lockout procedures, teach-mode restrictions, and customer-specific cell documentation that must be easy to find and follow.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can answer from approved safety procedures, training materials, OSHA references saved in your knowledge base, project-specific checklists, and internal review notes. For safety-critical questions, it can provide references and route to a qualified human instead of making the decision.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Customer-defined boundaries can limit the agent to retrieval, summaries, checklist navigation, and escalation for safety-sensitive workflows, with logs and role-based permissions supporting review.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Where is the risk-assessment template for this robot cell?
Which training document explains entering the robot work envelope?
Summarize the safety documentation gaps before customer handover.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Make safety and training references easier to access while keeping final safety judgments with qualified people.

agentUseCases.name: Documentation gap analytics for manuals and handovers
agentUseCases.problem: Robotics integrators produce large documentation sets, but customer questions reveal where manuals, training guides, and support pages still fail to answer real-world needs.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot logs conversations, topics, reports, satisfaction signals, resolution analysis, and unanswered questions. Teams can review recurring themes and update manuals, FAQs, onboarding content, and service knowledge bases accordingly.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Configured workflows can create documentation tasks, route content gaps to technical writers or engineers, and summarize trends for support or operations meetings.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What questions did customers ask most after the last deployment?
Which topics had low confidence or required human escalation?
Create a task to improve the gripper troubleshooting guide.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Turn support conversations into a practical improvement loop for documentation, training, and customer onboarding.

agentUseCases.name: Sales enablement for complex integration proposals
agentUseCases.problem: Account teams need fast, consistent answers about previous applications, proposal language, implementation constraints, service options, and objection handling.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can train on sales playbooks, service catalogs, proposal templates, case studies, implementation notes, pricing guidance, contract language, and customer FAQs. Reps can ask for grounded answers and draft follow-ups for human review.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved web search and Skills, agents can research public account context, summarize likely pain points, draft a follow-up email, or create an approved task in a sales workflow.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Draft a follow-up explaining our approach to machine tending ROI.
What objections should I prepare for a food-processing automation buyer?
Summarize the prospect’s public manufacturing footprint for my call notes.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Help sales teams respond faster while keeping commercial terms, legal language, and final outreach under human control.

customerFacingAgent.headline: Give customers and prospects a robotics support agent that knows your documentation
customerFacingAgent.intro: A DocsBot customer-facing agent can live on your website, documentation center, portal, or product support experience. It answers from approved content, collects missing details, supports multilingual questions, and escalates when the request needs an engineer, technician, or account owner.

requests.request: “What robot payload and reach do I need for this application?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from approved product, service, and qualification materials; ask clarifying questions about part weight, tooling, cycle time, reach, environment, and production goal; then route qualified opportunities to sales engineering.
requests.optionalAction: Submit a structured lead form or forward a qualified summary to a CRM, inbox, or sales queue through a configured workflow.

requests.request: “The system is stopped with an alarm. What should I check first?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Provide source-grounded troubleshooting guidance from the relevant manual or support article, ask for model and alarm details, and escalate urgent, site-specific, or safety-sensitive issues to human support.
requests.optionalAction: Create a support ticket, summarize attempted steps, and notify the support team when the customer has configured the action.

requests.request: “Where can I find the commissioning handover package?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Retrieve approved handover documents, training links, maintenance schedules, and customer onboarding resources from the documentation sources connected to the agent.
requests.optionalAction: Collect the project or cell identifier and route the request to the correct account or service team if the document is restricted.

requests.request: “Can you support our plant in another language?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Use DocsBot multilingual response capabilities to answer from the same approved knowledge base while preserving the company’s preferred support voice and escalation rules.
requests.optionalAction: Route language-specific leads or support requests to the right regional team through customer-configured workflows.

requests.request: “What safety documents should our operators review before using the cell?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Point to approved operator training, risk-assessment summaries, guarding procedures, and customer handover documents while avoiding unsupported safety judgments.
requests.optionalAction: Escalate site-specific safety questions to a qualified human and attach the conversation history for review.

requests.request: “Can I schedule a discovery call for a new automation project?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Collect scheduling intent and project basics such as application, production volume, facility location, timeline, and preferred follow-up channel.
requests.optionalAction: Use an approved scheduling workflow, such as Calendly, Cal.com, TidyCal, or a similar configured tool, to request or book a meeting.

internalAgent.headline: Give robotics teams a private knowledge agent for engineering, service, and sales
internalAgent.intro: DocsBot can operate as an internal knowledge assistant for robotics integrators, trained on private documents, SOPs, wikis, support notes, playbooks, project templates, and approved operational sources. Teams get fast, source-grounded answers while permissions, logs, and boundaries keep sensitive workflows controlled.

workflows.name: Field service troubleshooting
workflows.problem: Technicians need answers while they are in front of a robot cell, not after they search old folders or call a senior engineer.
workflows.agentWorkflow: The internal agent retrieves troubleshooting steps, alarm explanations, maintenance procedures, spare-part notes, and escalation rules from connected service knowledge.
workflows.optionalAction: Create a ticket, summarize the issue, or notify a service queue through approved Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflow automations.
workflows.outcome: Faster field response with better handoff context when the issue needs expert intervention.

workflows.name: Project delivery playbook access
workflows.problem: Project managers and engineers need consistent access to checklists, acceptance templates, change-order language, and commissioning processes.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers questions from internal playbooks, project templates, handover checklists, risk registers, and training materials, then points users back to the source documents.
workflows.optionalAction: Generate a summary, prepare a task, or route a missing-documentation request to the project owner when configured.
workflows.outcome: More consistent delivery processes across teams, sites, and repeatable applications.

workflows.name: Safety and training reference desk
workflows.problem: OSHA says integrators, operators, maintenance workers, and others near robot applications need to understand hazards and safeguards, making searchable training references important.
workflows.agentWorkflow: The agent helps employees locate approved training procedures, safeguarding references, lockout guidance, teach-mode notes, and internal safety review materials without replacing qualified safety review.
workflows.optionalAction: Escalate unresolved safety questions to the designated reviewer and include source links and the employee’s question history.
workflows.outcome: Make safety documentation more accessible while preserving human review for high-impact decisions.

workflows.name: Sales and proposal enablement
workflows.problem: Sales engineers need to respond quickly while staying aligned with approved service scope, standard proposal language, case studies, and implementation assumptions.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers rep questions from proposal templates, playbooks, case studies, customer FAQs, implementation notes, pricing guidance, and objection-handling content.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft a follow-up email, summarize account research, or create a sales task through approved workflows for human review.
workflows.outcome: Shorter response cycles for complex opportunities without letting AI finalize pricing, legal terms, or commitments.

docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: A robotics support agent can collect cell details, summarize downtime, create a support ticket, route a qualified lead, or trigger a documentation task when the customer has configured those workflows.

docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Prospects can share application type, payload, cycle-time goals, site constraints, timeline, and budget range before a robotics sales engineer enters the conversation.

trustClaims: Proven at high-volume support, including over 30,000 inquiries handled in one month for Sony NURO.
Built for teams that need AI answers grounded in their own technical content library.
Practical for technical organizations that want RAG-style knowledge access without building the stack themselves.
trustAndProof.whyTheseReviewsFit: Robotics integration buyers need credible AI that can handle technical volume, respect approved documentation, and help skilled teams move faster. DocsBot proof spans high-volume support, owned-content training, and technical teams choosing a managed knowledge-agent approach instead of maintaining custom RAG infrastructure.

schemaHints.serviceType: AI agents and workflow automation for robotics integration support, presales, documentation, and internal knowledge

OSHA robotics safety industrial robots hazards guidance

faq.question: Can DocsBot answer robot alarm and troubleshooting questions without giving unsafe advice?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can answer from approved manuals, service SOPs, troubleshooting guides, and support articles, then escalate safety-sensitive, site-specific, warranty, or high-impact issues to a human. Many teams configure the agent to collect robot model, cell ID, alarm code, operating context, and attempted fixes before handoff instead of improvising beyond approved sources.

faq.question: How do we keep the AI from mixing up procedures across different robot brands, cells, or customer sites?
faq.answer: The key is knowledge design and permissions. DocsBot can be trained on controlled source sets such as OEM manuals, commissioning packages, customer handover files, PLC notes, and SOPs. Teams can separate internal and customer-facing agents, restrict access with role-based permissions, and point answers back to the relevant source so users can verify which procedure, site, or document the answer came from.

faq.question: Can this help our field technicians when they are standing in front of a down robot cell?
faq.answer: DocsBot can serve as a private field-service knowledge agent for fast retrieval of alarm explanations, maintenance steps, spare-part notes, escalation rules, and commissioning history. If configured with approved Skills or APIs, it can also summarize the issue, create a support ticket, notify a service queue, or attach a handoff summary so senior engineers do not start from a blank page.

faq.question: What kinds of robotics documents can we train the agent on?
faq.answer: Common sources include robot manuals, EOAT notes, PLC conventions, IO maps, FAT and SAT checklists, risk-assessment templates, maintenance schedules, service SOPs, proposal templates, sales playbooks, case studies, customer FAQs, ticket exports, PDFs, Word documents, wikis, Google Drive folders, Notion, Confluence, and text files. The strongest deployments start with the documents teams already trust.

faq.question: Can DocsBot qualify new automation opportunities before sales engineering gets involved?
faq.answer: Yes. A presales agent can answer from approved service pages, case studies, specification sheets, FAQs, proposal templates, and sales playbooks, then collect structured details such as application, payload, reach, cycle-time target, product variation, facility constraints, timeline, budget range, and preferred follow-up channel. Qualified summaries can be routed to your CRM, inbox, form, or sales-engineering queue through customer-configured workflows.

faq.question: Will the agent calculate feasibility, ROI, or final pricing for a robot integration project?
faq.answer: DocsBot can help collect inputs, explain approved ROI assumptions, retrieve past proposal language, and draft follow-up notes for review. It should not be positioned as the final authority on feasibility, pricing, contractual terms, risk assessment, or guarantees. Robotics projects still need human sales engineers and applications engineers to validate payload, cycle time, tooling, safety, facility constraints, and commercial scope.

faq.question: How would we measure ROI for a robotics support AI agent?
faq.answer: Most integrators evaluate ROI through reduced repetitive support questions, faster first response, fewer interruptions to senior engineers, improved lead qualification, shorter time spent searching documentation, better handoff quality, and fewer documentation gaps after deployment. DocsBot analytics, logs, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and unanswered-question reporting help teams compare before-and-after support volume and knowledge gaps.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help us scale support as more robots are installed in the field?
faq.answer: Yes. As installed robot fleets grow, support teams face more questions about alarms, maintenance, handover documents, spare parts, and training. IFR reported 542,000 industrial robot installations worldwide in 2024, more than double the annual count from ten years earlier. DocsBot helps teams turn approved documentation into an always-available support layer while keeping escalations available for complex issues.

faq.question: Can customers use the agent on our website or portal without seeing internal project notes?
faq.answer: Yes. Customer-facing and internal agents should be separated by source selection and permissions. A public or portal agent can answer from approved customer documentation, FAQs, service pages, training guides, and handover materials, while private agents can use internal SOPs, ticket exports, proposal language, and engineering notes. Role-based access controls help keep proprietary and customer-specific information restricted.

faq.question: How does DocsBot handle safety documentation for robot cells?
faq.answer: DocsBot can make approved safety procedures, training materials, risk-assessment templates, guarding references, lockout guidance, and handover checklists easier to find. It should provide references and route unresolved or site-specific safety questions to qualified personnel. OSHA notes that workers may enter a robot working envelope during some operations, where unintended operations can result in injuries, so human review boundaries matter.

faq.question: Can the agent support operator training and onboarding after a cell handover?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can answer from customer handover packages, operator training guides, maintenance schedules, FAQs, and approved safety materials. It can help customers find the right document, clarify where procedures live, and route restricted or site-specific requests to the right account or service team. Conversation analytics can also reveal where onboarding content is unclear or missing.

faq.question: What if our documentation is incomplete, outdated, or scattered across project folders?
faq.answer: DocsBot works best when trained on approved sources, but it can also expose where documentation needs improvement. Its logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery can show repeated unanswered questions. Teams can use those insights to update manuals, commissioning packages, support articles, training guides, and FAQs over time.

faq.question: Can we use DocsBot internally for commissioning checklists and project delivery playbooks?
faq.answer: Yes. Internal teams can ask a private DocsBot agent about FAT and SAT checklists, commissioning steps, customer acceptance exceptions, change-order guidance, project templates, risk registers, and handover processes. The agent can point back to source documents and, when configured, create a task or route a missing-documentation request to the project owner.

faq.question: How does DocsBot fit with our ticketing, CRM, scheduling, or workflow tools?
faq.answer: DocsBot can use customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, forms, webhooks, workflow automations, and MCP Connectors to support approved actions such as creating tickets, routing requests, forwarding lead summaries, looking up record status, booking or requesting meetings, updating approved fields, or notifying teams. DocsBot should be connected only to scoped actions your team authorizes.

faq.question: Will DocsBot train public AI models on our robot cell designs, customer files, or internal procedures?
faq.answer: No. DocsBot does not use customer data to train public models. It also offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, and role-based permissions, which are important for integrators handling proprietary cell designs, customer documentation, support history, and internal engineering practices.

faq.question: Can the agent answer in languages used by our customers or regional service teams?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can answer in more than 100 languages from the same approved knowledge base. Robotics integrators can use this for customer portals, field support, operator training, and regional lead intake while preserving escalation rules and company-approved support language.

faq.question: How much human review should we keep in the workflow?
faq.answer: Keep humans in the loop for safety judgments, feasibility, pricing, contractual commitments, warranty decisions, site-specific troubleshooting, and anything that could affect equipment, people, or production. DocsBot is strongest as a retrieval, intake, drafting, summarization, and handoff layer: it gives teams better context faster while preserving expert control over high-impact decisions.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help sales engineers respond to complex proposal questions faster?
faq.answer: Yes. A sales enablement agent can answer from approved proposal templates, service catalogs, pricing guidance, case studies, implementation notes, objection-handling content, contract language, and customer FAQs. Reps can use it to draft follow-ups or prepare talking points, while humans remain responsible for final outreach, commercial terms, legal language, and technical commitments.

faq.question: Can this reduce interruptions to our senior controls engineers?
faq.answer: That is a common goal. DocsBot can answer repeat questions from SOPs, PLC notes, IO maps, commissioning guides, troubleshooting procedures, and prior project documentation so engineers are not constantly asked where something lives. When the question does require a senior engineer, the agent can collect details and summarize context before escalation.

faq.question: What reports would leadership get after launch?
faq.answer: DocsBot provides analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery. Leadership can review what customers, technicians, sales reps, or new hires ask most often; which topics trigger escalation; where confidence is low; and which manuals, FAQs, or training materials need improvement.

faq.question: How do we prevent the agent from answering questions outside our approved service scope?
faq.answer: You can constrain the agent with approved knowledge sources, instructions, role-based access, escalation rules, and customer-defined workflow boundaries. For example, it can answer standard maintenance and documentation questions, but route custom programming, safety validation, warranty disputes, or unsupported robot models to a human with a clear handoff summary.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for our helpdesk or knowledge base?
faq.answer: No. DocsBot is better viewed as an AI agent layer over your approved knowledge and workflows. It can answer from documentation, draft and summarize support responses, collect missing details, route escalations, and reveal knowledge gaps. Your helpdesk, knowledge base, CRM, file repositories, and human experts still remain the systems of record and decision makers.

faq.question: How difficult is implementation if our documents live in multiple systems?
faq.answer: Implementation usually starts by choosing the highest-value use case, then connecting or uploading the approved content needed for that agent. DocsBot can train on sources such as PDFs, Word documents, wikis, Google Drive, Notion, Confluence, text files, websites, and internal documents. Teams can phase rollout by starting with a support, presales, or internal knowledge agent before adding actions.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help with compliance and training expectations around robot hazards?
faq.answer: DocsBot can help employees and customers find approved training references, safeguarding procedures, lockout guidance, risk-assessment templates, and internal safety review materials. OSHA’s Technical Manual states that integrators, operators, maintenance workers, and others near robot applications need to understand robot hazards and how safeguards address them, so searchable, current training content is valuable.

faq.question: Why use DocsBot instead of building our own RAG system?
faq.answer: Many robotics integrators have the technical talent to build custom retrieval, but maintaining ingestion, permissions, prompts, analytics, evaluations, escalations, actions, and user interfaces can distract from billable engineering work. DocsBot gives teams a managed way to create source-grounded agents, use analytics, configure actions, and deploy internal or customer-facing experiences without owning the whole AI infrastructure stack.

faq.question: What should we launch first: customer support, internal engineering knowledge, or presales qualification?
faq.answer: Start where the same questions repeat and the source material is already trusted. Support is strong when manuals and SOPs are mature. Internal engineering knowledge is strong when teams waste time searching project folders. Presales is strong when website leads arrive with incomplete requirements. Many integrators begin with one focused agent, review analytics, then expand.
